title: Deogratias

cover.alt: Book cover showing a Black adolescent boy crouching on all fours in leafy bushes overlooking a lansdcape of hills and river after sunset.  In a star-filled sky is the text 'DEOGRATIAS / A TALE OF RWANDA / J. P. STASSEN'.

overview: Set in 1990s Rwanda around the time of the genocide there, this graphic novel follows a teenage Hutu boy who ends up unable either to successfully protect people he loves, or to avoid involvement in the atrocities.  Numerous incidents of violence or sex (or both) are implied, or mentioned in the often strongly crude dialogue, but generally occur before or after what is shown on the page. This artistic choice both makes the novel more suitable for adolescent readers, and reflects the title character's mental dissociation from his experiences and decisions. The book has won critical acclaim in multiple languages, with the original French version receiving the René Goscinny award, and the English version being named a 'Great Graphic Novel for Teens' by the American Library Association's Young Adult Library Services Association.

incidents.description: This was one of 16 young adult books that the Kootenai County library system {ln:https://www.spokesman.com/stories/2025/feb/18/kootenai-county-libraries-approved-every-relocatio/|moved to an area only allowed to adults} in response to requests to relocate it. As reported in the Spokesman-Review in February 2025, the library system moved all books for which they had received such requests except for {wk:olbp51209|the Bible}, and also proactively pulled {ln:https://www.spokesman.com/idaho-community-library-network-book-list/|another 140 titles} from the shelves to see if they should also be kept away from minors.
